title: lichen_ipv6_addr_to_str does not detect truncation
description:
snprintf return value is checked for encoding failure (ret < 0) but not for truncation. If ret >= buflen, output was truncated. The current code returns 0 (success) when truncation occurs.

Location:
- ipv6_addr.c:145-166 (lichen_ipv6_addr_to_str)

What goes wrong: If a caller passes a buffer smaller than documented (LICHEN_IPV6_ADDR_STR_LEN) but larger than 0, the function returns success with a truncated, potentially malformed IPv6 address string. Downstream code parsing or logging this string gets garbage.

Note: The buflen < LICHEN_IPV6_ADDR_STR_LEN check at line 137 prevents most practical cases, but the snprintf return check should be complete for defense in depth. Header doc also promises -ENOSPC on truncation.

Fix: Add check 'if (ret >= (int)buflen) { return -ENOSPC; }' after line 158.
